title: Limits of Recurrent Text Generation
concept: Because RNNs process sequentially and forget long-term context, generated text eventually drifts into nonsense or repetitive loops. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: NLP Basics Capstone.'

exercises.prompt: What is the main limitation of Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) when generating text?

options.text: They forget early words as the text gets longer.
They can only write one sentence per day.

exercises.explain: Because RNNs process text step-by-step, their memory of earlier words fades over time, leading to errors in long texts.

cards.title: The Whispering Machine
cards.body: Have you ever played the game of 'Telephone', where a secret whisper gets passed down a line of people? By the time it reaches the last person, "I love reading books" often becomes "I shove feeding ducks." Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) suffer from this exact problem when trying to write long paragraphs!

cards.title: What is Recurrent Decay?
cards.body: Recurrent text generation happens when an AI generates text one word at a time, feeding its own previous words back into itself. Because RNNs pass information step-by-step through a single memory lane, early details fade away. This limited memory window causes the generated text to drift into repetitive loops or pure nonsense over long runs.

cards.title: The Photo-Copier Effect
cards.body: Imagine copy-pasting a sentence over and over using a broken copier. Each time you copy a copy, the text gets a tiny bit blurry. By the 10th copy, the words smudge together, and by the 50th copy, it is just random ink blobs. RNNs do this with memory; they copy current context to the next step, losing details along the way until the text becomes gibberish.

cards.title: A Story Lost in Space
cards.body: If you ask an RNN to write a story about a boy named Leo who went to space, it might start strong: "Leo put on his space suit." But after 20 words, its memory of "Leo" and "space" fades. It might end up repeating: "and he went to the store and he went to the store and he went..." because it forgot the original prompt.

cards.title: The Sentence vs. The Essay
cards.body: You might think that because an AI can generate a perfect next word, it can easily write a 10-page essay. In reality, while RNNs are great at predicting the very next word, they struggle to plan a whole story. Without a global plan, they quickly lose their train of thought.

cards.title: Memory Over Time
cards.body: Let's look at how memory behaves in sequential RNN generation compared to what we actually want.

left.text: Fades quickly. The start of the sentence is completely forgotten after a few steps.

right.text: Stays constant. The AI remembers the topic from the first word to the very last word.

exercises.prompt: What typically happens when an older RNN tries to write a very long essay?

options.text: The AI runs out of words in its vocabulary dictionary.
The AI is programmed to stop writing after exactly ten words.
The AI forgets the early context and gets stuck in repetitive loops.
The text becomes too fast for the computer to display.

exercises.explain: Because RNNs process step-by-step and have limited memory retention, they tend to forget the original topic and start repeating words or phrases.

exercises.prompt: True or False: RNNs generate long text easily because they process all words in parallel rather than sequentially.
exercises.explain: RNNs process text sequentially (word-by-word), which is exactly why they struggle with long-term memory compared to newer parallel architectures.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe why recurrent models lose track of long texts.
exercises.sentence: RNNs struggle with long text generation because their sequential memory fades over time.
exercises.explain: RNNs pass information step-by-step. Over many steps, this memory link weakens, causing the model to forget how the text started.

exercises.prompt: Which of these texts shows the classic 'repetitive loop' error common in simple recurrent generation?

options.text: "Once upon a time, a young girl named Lily found a hidden key in the garden, which opened a secret wooden chest in her attic."
"The dog barked at the car and the car barked at the dog and the dog barked at the car and the dog barked."
"Deep learning models require large amounts of data to recognize complex visual patterns in images."

exercises.explain: AI-generated text from simple recurrent networks often displays repetitive loops (like "and then... and then...") because it lacks long-term context tracking.

exercises.prompt: Why do Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) struggle to generate long, coherent articles without losing the plot?

options.text: They process words in parallel, making them too fast to keep track of grammar.
They process step-by-step and have a fading memory of early words.
They lack the ability to predict the very next word in a sequence.
They use too much long-term memory, causing them to get stuck on the first word.

exercises.explain: RNNs read and write word-by-word. Without a way to look back at the entire document at once, they quickly lose track of the beginning, leading to repetitive loops or total gibberish. Next up: NLP Basics Capstone.
